name: Mi Lan Xiang
terroir: Wu Dong Shan, mid to high elevation, subtropical mountain climate
flavor_profile: The most iconic Dan Cong — thick, sweet, and unmistakably orchid. Extended oxidation and traditional roasting produce a honey-forward cup with layered floral depth. Viscous mouthfeel with a lingering sweetness that lingers on the palate for hours.

brewing.rinse_hint: Brief 5-second rinse — Mi Lan Xiang opens quickly.
brewing.tips: Dan Cong demands attention: a few seconds too long and bitterness creeps in. Reward careful brewing with one of the most aromatic teas you will ever drink.
